decisions.title: Keep business rules in backend services
decisions.decision: Permissions, validations, workflow rules, state transitions, and automation assumptions belong in backend services so web and future mobile clients share the same behavior.

decisions.title: Keep controllers transport-only
decisions.decision: Controllers should stay thin and delegate behavior, DTO assembly, and permission decisions to service or mapper layers.

decisions.title: Logic changes move with living docs
decisions.decision: A logic change is not complete when only code and tests are updated; affected docs, agent artifacts, and validation tests move in the same change.

decisions.title: Generated source-of-truth artifacts must stay fresh
decisions.decision: Generated endpoint, read-model, backend-audit, source-of-truth, and frontend contract artifacts are authoritative review inputs when their source surfaces change.

decisions.title: Schema changes use new Flyway migrations
decisions.decision: Schema changes add a new Flyway migration instead of editing old migrations.

decisions.title: Sandbox behavior stays separate
decisions.decision: Sandbox and synthetic admin-generation behavior must stay explicitly separated from production-like user flows and semantics.

decisions.title: No commit or push by default
decisions.decision: Do not commit or push changes unless the user explicitly requests it.

decisions.title: High-risk work needs plan-backed closeout
decisions.decision: Multi-file, multi-layer, high-risk, master-plan-driven, or manifest-backed changes need explicit plan evidence, validation evidence, backlog hygiene, and closeout checks.

decisions.title: Centralize operational config
decisions.decision: Operational backend settings such as TTLs, cleanup jobs, limits, polling intervals, and timeouts belong in typed central configuration objects.

decisions.title: Standardize authenticated frontend surfaces
decisions.decision: Authenticated pages should use shared app shell, shared components, shared tokens, and minimal UI-side product logic.
